spec.doc: Parcel Post and Express Post - Label & Barcode Specification v1.4

rules.title: Label size 15 x 10 cm
rules.description: The label must measure 150 x 100 mm in either orientation, within print tolerance.

messages.pass: Page dimensions match the 150 x 100 mm label size ({actual}).
messages.fail: Page dimensions do not match the specified 150 x 100 mm label size ({actual}).
messages.missing: Physical dimensions could not be determined from this file. Review whether the label is 150 x 100 mm.

rules.title: Label generation date (MMDD) printed
rules.description: The MMDD date of label generation must be printed at the expected position on the label.

messages.pass: A standalone MMDD date value was found on the label: {actual}.
messages.missing: No standalone MMDD date was extracted from the label text. Confirm the label generation date is printed in the expected position.

rules.title: Dangerous goods declaration
rules.description: The Aviation Security / Dangerous Goods declaration must appear on the label. Small-quantity DG labels instead carry FOR ROAD TRANSPORT ONLY.

messages.pass: Dangerous goods declaration text is present.
messages.fail: Dangerous goods declaration was not confirmed from extracted text.

rules.title: TO address block present
rules.description: The delivery block must open with the keyword TO or DELIVER TO.

messages.pass: TO address block text was extracted.
messages.missing: TO/DELIVER TO was not found or could not be isolated from the text layer.

rules.title: TO suburb, state, postcode line
rules.description: The last line of the TO block must hold suburb, state and postcode on one line, fully capitalised, with no commas.

messages.pass: Suburb, state and postcode line is correctly formatted: {actual}.
messages.fail: Suburb/state/postcode line must be on one line, fully capitalised, with no commas. Found: {actual}.

rules.title: FROM address block present
rules.description: The sender block must open with the keyword FROM or SENDER.

messages.pass: FROM address block text was extracted.
messages.missing: FROM/SENDER was not found or could not be isolated from the text layer.

rules.title: FROM suburb, state, postcode line
rules.description: The last line of the FROM block must hold suburb, state and postcode on one line, fully capitalised, with no commas.

messages.pass: Sender suburb, state and postcode line is correctly formatted: {actual}.
messages.fail: Sender suburb/state/postcode line must be on one line, fully capitalised, with no commas. Found: {actual}.

rules.title: GS1-128 linear barcode decoded
rules.description: A linear GS1-128 barcode must be present and decodable.

messages.pass: Required GS1-128 linear barcode was decoded from the uploaded file.

rules.title: Source image resolution
rules.description: Raster label images need roughly 300 DPI for reliable linear barcode decoding. Low-resolution exports collapse the narrow bars and spaces, destroying the encoded data.

messages.pass: Estimated image resolution is about {actual} DPI, which should be sufficient for barcode decoding.
messages.fail: Estimated image resolution is about {actual} DPI (assuming a standard 100mm label edge). Linear barcodes are typically unrecoverable below about 200 DPI - upload the original PDF or export the label at 300 DPI or higher.

rules.title: GS1 DataMatrix barcode decoded
rules.description: A GS1 DataMatrix barcode must be present and decodable.

messages.pass: Required GS1 DataMatrix barcode was decoded from the uploaded file.

rules.title: AI 01 Australia Post GTIN
rules.description: GS1 barcodes must begin with AI 01 followed by the Australia Post GTIN 99312650999998.

messages.pass: Barcode begins with AI 01 and the expected Australia Post GTIN.
messages.fail: Barcode has AI 01 but does not match the expected Australia Post GTIN 99312650999998.

rules.title: AI 91 article component
rules.description: AI 91 must follow the Australia Post GTIN and introduce the article identifier.

messages.pass: AI 91 article component was found after the GTIN.
messages.fail: AI 91 was not found after the Australia Post GTIN prefix.

rules.title: Linear and DataMatrix encode the same article
rules.description: The GS1-128 linear barcode and the GS1 DataMatrix must carry the same article number.

rules.title: Article ID parse
rules.description: Decoded barcode data must yield a valid article identifier: 21 or 23 character eParcel article, or 20-digit SSCC including AI 00.

messages.pass: Valid article identifier(s) parsed from decoded barcode data.
messages.fail: No valid article identifier could be extracted from decoded barcode data.

rules.title: MLID format
rules.description: The Merchant Location ID must be 3 or 5 uppercase alphanumeric characters.

rules.title: Consignment serial
rules.description: The consignment serial following the MLID must be exactly 7 digits.

rules.title: Article count 01-20
rules.description: The article count is the position of the article within the consignment and must be between 01 and 20.

rules.title: Postage paid indicator
rules.description: The postage paid indicator is the fixed value 0.

rules.title: eParcel check digit
rules.description: The article check digit must satisfy the EAN/UCC-13 algorithm with alpha characters converted to the last digit of their ASCII code.

rules.title: Visible consignment matches barcode
rules.description: The visible Cons No must match the consignment embedded in the decoded article. Text extraction is heuristic and may be affected by watermarks or label overlays.

messages.pass: Visible Cons No matches the decoded barcode consignment.
messages.fail: Visible Cons No does not match the decoded barcode consignment. Confirm visually — text extraction may be affected by watermarks or overlays.

rules.title: Visible article ID matches barcode
rules.description: The human-readable AP Article ID must equal the decoded barcode article once spacing is removed. Text extraction is heuristic and may be affected by watermarks or label overlays.

messages.pass: Visible AP Article ID matches the decoded barcode article.
messages.fail: Visible AP Article ID does not match the decoded barcode article. Confirm visually — text extraction may be affected by watermarks or overlays.

rules.title: Known service code
rules.description: The two-digit service code must be a published eParcel service code.

rules.title: Known product code
rules.description: The five-character product code must be a published eParcel product code.

rules.title: Service and product compatibility
rules.description: The service code must support the product code per the service options matrix.

rules.title: Product matches the audited label type
rules.description: The product code embedded in the barcode must belong to the product family selected for this audit.

rules.title: FNC1 group separator encoding
rules.description: Group separators must be encoded as control characters (ASCII 29), never as literal text such as $, _1 or FNC1.

messages.pass: No invalid literal FNC1/group separator markers were found.
messages.fail: Invalid literal separator marker detected. The GS1 FNC1/group separator must be a control character, not printed text.

rules.title: AI 420 delivery postcode
rules.description: The DataMatrix must contain AI 420 followed by a four-digit delivery postcode.

messages.pass: AI 420 postcode is present: {actual}.
messages.fail: AI 420 delivery postcode is missing or invalid: {actual}.
messages.missing: AI 420 delivery postcode is missing from the DataMatrix payload.

rules.title: AI 420 postcode matches the TO address
rules.description: The postcode encoded in the DataMatrix must equal the postcode visible in the TO address block.

rules.title: AI 92 Delivery Point Identifier
rules.description: When AI 92 is present it must carry an 8-digit DPID other than 00000000. When the DPID is unavailable, AI 92 and its separator must be omitted entirely.

messages.pass: DPID is present and valid: {actual}.
messages.fail: DPID is present but invalid ({actual}). If unavailable, omit AI 92 and its separator entirely.

rules.title: AI 92 omitted (DPID unavailable)
rules.description: Omitting AI 92 and its separator is the correct encoding when no DPID is available.

messages.pass: DPID is absent. This is allowed when AI 92 and its separator are omitted.

rules.title: AI 8008 label generation date/time
rules.description: The DataMatrix must contain AI 8008 followed by YYMMDDHHMMSS with no spaces and a valid calendar date/time.

messages.pass: AI 8008 date/time is present and plausible: {actual}.
messages.fail: AI 8008 label generation date/time is missing or invalid: {actual}.
messages.missing: AI 8008 label generation date/time is missing from the DataMatrix payload.
